summary: Activated iterative-dev-logger for graduation experiment planning and created missing memory/events.jsonl.
why: Project instructions require layered memory for multi-step experiment work.
verification: read skill instructions and project files
next: Update dev_log.md with the new experiment-planning task and proposed comparison matrix.

summary: Implemented new dual_axis_memory attention with row-wise causal self-attention plus column-wise same-token depth retrieval using K=V=x from previous layer outputs.
why: User requested a concrete implementation of the proposed horizontal-plus-vertical retrieval architecture for direct comparison against baseline and attn_residuals.
verification: local assoc_recall smoke test on CPU completed successfully
next: Run a first three-way comparison on associative recall and inspect whether the new path is numerically stable.

summary: Ran initial three-way CPU associative-recall comparison for baseline, attn_residuals, and dual_axis_memory at 100 steps on a small shared config.
why: Needed an immediate sanity check that the new architecture trains and produces comparable artifacts before moving to larger server runs.
verification: all three runs completed successfully on CPU
next: Promote the comparison to a more informative server-scale configuration and decide whether dual_axis_memory needs a gate or normalization tweak.

summary: Synced updated training/model files to the default Windows server, verified a remote CUDA smoke run for dual_axis_memory, and launched a larger sequential three-way associative-recall comparison over baseline, attn_residuals, and dual_axis_memory.
why: User requested that the experiments be run on the server with larger batch size and training budget.
verification: remote smoke run completed successfully on cuda; long-running compare session started over ssh
next: Poll the remote session and collect artifacts assoc_recall_baseline_server_dualaxis_b64_s800.json, assoc_recall_attn_residuals_server_dualaxis_b64_s800.json, and assoc_recall_dual_axis_memory_server_dualaxis_b64_s800.json when finished.

summary: Completed the larger server-side three-way associative-recall comparison. All three methods reached 1.0 final accuracy, with attn_residuals converging earliest, dual_axis_memory second, and baseline last.
why: Needed a stronger result than the local CPU probe to judge whether the new dual-axis architecture is competitive.
verification: remote server run completed successfully for all three JSON artifacts
next: Compare sample efficiency explicitly at step 400/500/600 and decide whether to move the same three-way comparison onto WikiText next.

summary: Started a sequential WikiText-2 comparison on the Windows server for baseline, attn_residuals, and dual_axis_memory using the same LM training entrypoint and a shared 1000-step configuration.
why: User requested the same three-way comparison on a standard text benchmark to see whether the ranking matches associative recall.
verification: remote python process active on server under the requested command

summary: Completed the WikiText-2 three-way comparison. attn_residuals ranked first, baseline second, and dual_axis_memory last on both validation and test metrics at 1000 steps.
why: Needed to verify whether the associative-recall ranking generalizes to standard language modeling.
verification: all three remote JSON artifacts were read successfully
next: Summarize the divergence between associative recall and WikiText, then decide whether to tune dual_axis_memory or shift the thesis claim toward task-dependent advantages.

summary: Completed two placement ablations on WikiText-2: causal-only dual_axis_memory and residual-only attn_residuals_dual_axis. Both underperformed baseline and the original attn_residuals.
why: User requested separate experiments for changing only causal attention versus changing only residual attention.
verification: both remote JSON artifacts completed successfully
next: Compare the two placement ablations against baseline and original attn_residuals, then decide whether a gated variant is needed.

summary: Verified that WikiText-2 with seq_len=512 runs on the 4060 Ti at batch_size=4 and grad_accum_steps=2 using a short baseline probe.
why: Needed a safe memory-fit configuration before launching the longer seq_len=512 comparison runs.
verification: 2-step remote baseline probe completed successfully on cuda
next: Launch the longer seq_len=512 comparison runs for the current distinct method set and record each run.

summary: Launched the longer WikiText-2 seq_len=512 comparison runs for baseline, attn_residuals, dual_axis_memory, and attn_residuals_dual_axis in one sequential remote job.
why: User requested rerunning the longer-context text experiments and asked that every experiment be recorded.
verification: remote sequential ssh session started successfully; remote python process confirmed active
next: Poll for each finished artifact and append the resulting metrics to project memory.

summary: Added the fifth seq_len=512 experiment dual_axis_full, representing the user's main version that modifies both causal attention and residual attention. Queued it to start automatically after the current four-run remote sequence finishes.
why: User clarified that the main method is the full version with both causal and residual paths modified, so the seq512 comparison set should contain five experiments rather than four.
verification: local smoke test for dual_axis_full passed; remote queue script uploaded and launched
next: After the first four seq512 runs complete, collect the fifth dual_axis_full artifact as part of the final longer-context comparison table.

summary: Collected the first four seq_len=512 WikiText-2 results and found that the queued dual_axis_full run had not started automatically, so launched it manually as a standalone fifth run.
why: Needed to inspect completed seq512 metrics and ensure the fifth experiment actually runs.
verification: first four JSON artifacts read successfully; standalone dual_axis_full remote session started
next: Collect the final dual_axis_full result and fold all five metrics into one longer-context comparison summary.
